2604.18574.title: When Can LLMs Learn to Reason with Weak Supervision?
2604.18574.paper_summary: Large language models have achieved significant reasoning improvements through reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR). Yet as model capabilities grow, constructing high-quality reward signals becomes increasingly difficult, making it essential to understand when RLVR can succeed under weaker forms of supervision. We conduct a systematic empirical study across diverse model families and reasoning domains under three weak supervision settings: scarce data, noisy rewards, and self-supervised proxy rewards. We find that generalization is governed by training reward saturation dynamics: models that generalize exhibit a prolonged pre-saturation phase during which training reward and downstream performance climb together, while models that saturate rapidly memorize rather than learn. We identify reasoning faithfulness, defined as the extent to which intermediate steps logically support the final answer, as the pre-RL property that predicts which regime a model falls into, while output diversity alone is uninformative. Motivated by these findings, we disentangle the contributions of continual pre-training and supervised fine-tuning, finding that SFT on explicit reasoning traces is necessary for generalization under weak supervision, while continual pre-training on domain data amplifies the effect. Applied together to Llama3.2-3B-Base, these interventions enable generalization across all three settings where the base model previously failed.
2604.18574.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型透過可驗證獎勵的強化學習 (RLVR) 實現了顯著的推理改進。<lb/>然而，隨著模型能力的增長，構建高質量的獎勵信號變得越來越困難，因此理解在較弱的監督形式下何時 RLVR 可以成功變得至關重要。<lb/>我們在三種弱監督設置下進行了跨多樣模型系列和推理領域的系統實證研究：稀缺數據、噪聲獎勵和自我監督代理獎勵。<lb/>我們發現，泛化受訓練獎勵飽和動態的支配：能夠泛化的模型在訓練獎勵和下游性能一起上升的延長預飽和階段中表現，而快速飽和的模型則是記憶而非學習。<lb/>我們確定推理的忠實性，定義為中間步驟在多大程度上邏輯上支持最終答案，作為預 RL 屬性，預測模型落入哪種情境，而僅僅是輸出多樣性則無法提供信息。<lb/>受到這些發現的啟發，我們解開了持續預訓練和監督微調的貢獻，發現對明確推理痕跡的 SFT 是在弱監督下實現泛化所必需的，而在領域數據上的持續預訓練則放大了這一效果。<lb/>將這些干預措施應用於 Llama3.2-3B-Base，使其能夠在之前失敗的所有三種設置中實現泛化。
2604.18574.author: Salman Rahman et.al.
2604.18574.authors: Salman Rahman, Jingyan Shen, Anna Mordvina, Hamid Palangi, Saadia Gabriel, Pavel Izmailov
